etymology.description: semantic 行(行) (háng, "street") + phonetic 𩠐 + semantic 止(止) (zhǐ, "foot") -> 亍 (street) and 止 (foot) combined into 辵 (walk; movement) in the seal script
etymology.interpretation: The character originally combined the idea of a street 行(行) (háng, "street") with 止(止) (zhǐ, "foot") indicating footsteps, supported by the sound from 𩠐. This depicted the notion of walking down a street or path, leading to the meanings of 'path' and 'way'. As a path represents an underlying guidance or method, it naturally extended to mean 'method' and 'teaching'.

examples.chinese: 他了解许多成功的道理(他了解許多成功的道理)。
examples.pinyin: Tā liǎojiě xǔduō chénggōng de dàolǐ.
examples.english: He understands many principles of success.

examples.chinese: 这条路是通往山顶的唯一道路(這條路是通往山頂的唯一道路)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo lù shì tōngwǎng shāndǐng de wéiyī dàolù.
examples.english: This path is the only way to the mountain top.

examples.chinese: 老子传授了很多关于道的哲学(老子傳授了很多關於道的哲學)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎozǐ chuánshòu le hěn duō guānyú dào de zhéxué.
examples.english: Laozi imparted much philosophy about the Tao.
